name: Bangladesh Kurma or Korma
recipeIngredient: 1 1/2 lb Chicken breast, skinned and cubed
6 tablespoons Butter, ghee or vegetable oil
1 teaspoon Saffron ground or yellow food coloring
recipeInstructions:
1. In a small bowl, mix the saffron with milk and set aside.
2. Heat butter or oil in a large pan over medium heat. Add chicken cubes and turn frequently until browned on all sides, about 2 minutes.
3. Add garlic puree and stir-fry for 1 minute. Then add onion puree and stir-fry for another 3 minutes to remove moisture content.
4. Stir in garam masala and fry for an additional 2 minutes. The total frying time should be around 10 minutes, with the chicken being partially cooked.
5. Add yogurt, cream, and ground almonds. Reduce heat to low and simmer, stirring occasionally, for another 10 minutes until the chicken is fully cooked. Adjust seasoning if needed.
6. Strain saffron mixture through a fine sieve into a small bowl, using warm water to extract color.
7. Taste and adjust salt before serving. Garnish with chopped coriander leaves and whole roasted almonds. Serve immediately.
